localizedDescriptions.en: Generic encounter or progress note composition
Problem-oriented heading in the record of the problem presented by the patient or client
The subjective findings or story told by the person.
A structure to collect information provided by the person or information provider in relation to the symptoms and difficulties they are presenting to the clinician
Information provided about the person
An issue, symptom, or complaint presented to the health provider seeking explanation, education, understanding, investigation or treatment
The issue as presented by the person
Detailed information about the issue or health problem
A symptom experienced by the patient
The symptom experienced
The features of the symptom
Description of the symptom
The severity of the symptom
the symptom is trivial and causes no problems
the symptom does not interfere greatly with day to day activities
the symptom interferes with day to day activities
the symptom prohibits some key daily activities
the symptom is almost maximum possible intensity and prohibits any activity
Measures of the intensity of the symptom at this time
The degree the symptom is bothering the patient
the symptom is not present
the symptom is trivial and causes no problems
the symptom does not interfere greatly with day to day activities
the symptom interferes with day to day activities
the symptom prohibits some key daily activities
the symptom is almost maximum possible intensity and prohibits any activity
The duration of the symptom since onset
The character of the symptom
The variation of the symptom
The symptom is constantly present
The symptom is present at times and not at others
The symptom varys considerably but is almost always present
Factors that trigger or bring on the symptom
An event or activity that brings on or triggers the symptom
Factors that change the level of intensity of the symptom
Information about a specific factor that changes the symptom
An event or activity that modifies the symptom
An event or activity that makes the symptom worse
The symptom is relieved by this factor
The symptom is made better by this factor
The symptom is somewhat improved by this factor
The symptom is not modified by this factor
The symptom is made somewhat worse by this factor
The symptom is made worse by this factor
The symptom is made much worse by this factor
Details of the effect
The objective findings or observations made by the clinician.

The local measurement of arterial blood pressure which is a surrogate for arterial. pressure in the systemic circulation.  Most commonly, use of the term 'blood pressure' refers to measurement of brachial artery pressure in the upper arm.
Default event
Peak systemic arterial blood pressure  - measured in systolic or contraction phase of the heart cycle.
Minimum systemic arterial blood pressure - measured in the diastolic or relaxation phase of the heart cycle.
Comment on blood pressure measurement.
The position of the subject at the time of measurement.
Standing at the time of blood pressure measurement.
Sitting (for example on bed or chair) at the time of blood pressure measurement.
Lying flat at the time of blood pressure measurement.
The size of the cuff used for blood pressure measurement.
A cuff used for an adult thigh - bladder approx 20cm x 42cm.
A cuff for adults with larger arms - bladder approx 16cm x 38cm.
A cuff that is standard for an adult - bladder approx 13cm x 30cm.
A cuff used for a small adult - bladder approx 10cm x 24cm.

annotations.comment: Perloff D, Grim C, Flack J, Frohlich ED, Hill M, McDonald M, Morgenstern BZ. Human blood pressure determination by sphygmomanometry. Circulation 1993;88;2460-2470.

localizedDescriptions.en: The mechanical heart rate  - usually measured by palpation
The rate of the heart on palpation
The rate of the heart as beats per minute
The rhythm of the heart beat
Regular heart beat
The heart is irregular
Irregular in a chaotic manner
Double beats with intervening gap
Triple beat with intervening gap
Missed beats less than 2 per minute
Missed beats of greater than 2 per minute
A measurement of the body temperature, which is a surrogate for the whole body temperature of the person.
Any event
The measured body temperature (as a surrogate for the whole body)
The observed characteristics of spontaneous breathing as would commonly be recorded as part of a 'vital signs' examination.
Generic event
Rate of respiration.
Rhythm of respiration.
Regular respiration.
Irregular respirations.
Depth of respiration.
Normal depth of breathing.
Shallow depth of breathing.
Deep breathing.
Variable depth of breathing.
A text description of respirations.
The assessment made by the clinician.
A diagnosis defined by a clinician which is coded in an accepted terminology and may include the stage of the condition and the diagnostic criteria
The index diagnosis
The status of the diagnosis
provisional diagnosis considered likely and a basis for proceeding with management
working diagnosis considered very likely but not yet confirmed
Plans made or procedures carried out.
Request for imaging services
Information about the imaging action(s) to be carried out
Clinical information relevant to the imaging investigation
